textByHashes.52c2544e08d8dcb3680f4434a15035ae16137614: That's right. What can I do for you?
textByHashes.dac807b5e550202f388792be96cfe3ddedf6ed08: Ah yes. To be honest, not much is known about them. The sect was destroyed long ago. However, it is said that this temple was once their home.
textByHashes.151a3b76615747ec0854c3036194c7a723110a6b: It was just before the plagues started! They burst up from under the cellar and trashed the place! It was late at night, so I couldn't see who they were, or if they were even human!
textByHashes.a53df9e33aeb44534dc9477c3d76f048d8029639: The first of the plagues started that next morning. It didn't seem wise to bother him.
textByHashes.4b4a47be8130f69350df80808469ea2cbb5b6026: That's right. Turns out there's quite the network of tunnels down there.
textByHashes.f83c50fbacb75cbf3233f502481ae4259c368033: I wouldn't if I were you. It's incredibly dangerous down there. We had a quick look, but there are some fearsome traps and vicious monsters lurking in the darkness.
textByHashes.6d4bde580e27d61d70fb8a833423f64123516c97: Well then you're either very brave or very foolish. We covered the tunnel they made with a trapdoor. Just head down into the cellar and then down that trapdoor.
textByHashes.e4c0055cccbb07d38375f68985f3b7a84a7efddb: Oh, it's also very dark down there. You should take some sort of light source and a tinderbox with you.
textByHashes.ad70f5146aa061c139beb6dc7ccb76027b49ebb9: Well don't forget to take a light source and a tinderbox with you.
textByHashes.af835465e9c7d054b009e3a5acffa8e17b6a666e: Some degenerate clubbed me and gave me a lump the size of an egg on the back of my head.
textByHashes.c601e73596879bd239356131aab557a0b0693659: I have a fairly good idea who did it.
textByHashes.fbfd4a2486e7f6479a06680102ef311e75d53a17: That linen merchant, Siamun. He's never liked me.
textByHashes.388b3ce71c2c4c2658d2b3468972df9c55a407db: I will not speak with a defiler of the dead. Be gone!
textByHashes.10b9cbfb1ab4536813719f29a1d2d7dd5d7e06c1: Hey! You don't have permission to go down there.
textByHashes.e441cd733dd45fafe65e5bc00d9f030abc4906c2: Hello there. Welcome to the Temple of the Lesser Gods.
textByHashes.deb68aaa52de67ace486b7f66f486b4ca96b390b: Of course. The lesser gods, or avatars as some call them, were born from the dreams of Tumeken. They are Apmeken, Het, Crondis and Scabaras.
textByHashes.42c51f0463664b4fd2a2681979d214f3dffda5c4: Apmeken is the goddess of companionship. She is known to be the most mischievous, playful and unpredictable of the minor deities. I'd say she's my personal favourite.
textByHashes.146fd37210ac04d35475a45994320588fe55b83d: Apmeken can appeal to the most erudite scholar or the most barefaced liar. I'd like to be considered amongst the former category.
textByHashes.1c134132dc693a0300c3575e325f8279038b05bd: She has the head of a monkey, or sometimes an ape. It changes depending upon what nature she is displaying. For example, the orangutan represents wisdom, while the baboon represents mischief.
textByHashes.e36f61852d91f8a488be7d474b654059837cf867: Het is the least strange of our deities to foreign eyes, for he looks just like us. I suspect this is why his shrines are particularly targeted by thieves like that infamous Templeton.
textByHashes.e3fa1ae55d65a2709d4480ca65c400dc157dbb67: Het is the god of health, both in mind and body. He is quite popular amongst soldiers as a result, especially up in Al Kharid.
textByHashes.6f30899094d9dfd50dd8e2c623515fd677ce44cf: Not at all! Like I said, the wellbeing of the mind is also key to Het. Many great orators also look to him for guidance. I imagine his teachings help with some of their strenuous speeches.
textByHashes.d9336f6b2d02cfa1858ce66ae7f2db199e8d6d89: With many arguments. They really couldn't decide whether it was a punishment from Het, or an affront to him. I stayed out of that debate.
textByHashes.0fc62c6ba1244d064c758f8b93efd25ace8f0c7e: Crondis is a ferocious lady with the head of a mighty crocodile.
textByHashes.f21d215aad0845e9b1858fc19fd2d843171fae8e: Luckily for her, I don't think deities have much need for them.
textByHashes.1baf9f2062fa4e9bdb7075bbbf8dd9f2be300586: Resourcefulness. A word which seems to be open to large interpretation.
textByHashes.0209f78523e10e0831b004b980825f0ecd35da33: Most consider her to be a teacher of modesty. They say we should be resourceful by only taking and using that which we absolutely need.
textByHashes.ae33c9bd37d26cae140b14706c83b3c2fee12760: Some believe she is actually the goddess of pleasure. They claim that the idea of modesty came from a misinterpretation, and that to be resourceful is to take all that you can.
textByHashes.2fd2745302f64b18802fcdc75605c03a238bd35e: No. It is a more recent take that I suspect was born in Menaphos. There are many in that city with more wealth than they'll ever need. Modesty is not an ideal they'll ever embody.
textByHashes.1acb1c6de87a62d218b3718c07db04f3c6d809a3: There are many opinions out there on Scabaras, but general knowledge isn't always the most accurate.
textByHashes.eebfb3fe03efc48a09ee2ad1c023a1746c027267: Scabaras is the god of isolation. He takes on the appearance of a man with the head of a scarab. Both he and his followers were driven into hiding long ago.
textByHashes.87076604876480a4f6a8c50bf5779874cf2eefea: And that's it. You asked for the absolute facts. Anything beyond that is just speculation.
textByHashes.9abbaa1fcbacb86befd0c9c2b2ae0a5e32e50df6: So it is said, but the written accounts that exist contain many inconsistencies when it comes to what role Scabaras actually played. Of course, most would not know that.
textByHashes.44bab519c7bade6a85f0177463cf9a16a0b99db0: Many of the writings are only available to us priests. That does of course include the new High Priest, but perhaps he is yet to read them. They are definitely... illuminating.
